identity.name: Robert Wilson's Moby Dick at BAM

narrative.summary:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

30 Lafayette Ave, Fort Greene — Brooklyn Academy of Music, the Peter Jay Sharp Building

Robert Wilson's final major production. Melville's Moby Dick reimagined as avant-garde theater with indie-rock score by Anna Calvi. In German and English with supertitles. April 29–May 3, 2026.

blocks.title: Arrival
blocks.body:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

You're approaching the grand Beaux-Arts facade of BAM's Peter Jay Sharp Building on Lafayette Ave in Fort Greene. This is hallowed ground for anyone who cares about adventurous performance — the place where Philip Glass, Pina Bausch, and Robert Wilson himself have been pushing boundaries since the 1970s. Tonight, you're here for what may be the most significant theatrical event of the season.

blocks.title: The Production
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗗𝗨𝗖𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡

Robert Wilson (1941–2025) — one of the most important theater artists of the past century — created this production for Germany's Düsseldorfer Schauspielhaus. It's his take on Melville's great American novel, and it's unmistakably Wilson: poetically deliberate use of time, space, and light. Character designs that evoke 1920s silent films. The story becomes less about a whale and more about humanity's capacity for destruction and rebirth.

Wilson's long relationship with BAM began with The Life and Times of Sigmund Freud in 1970. This production — presented by BAM and Dance Reflections by Van Cleef & Arpels — continues that legacy.

🎵 Music by Anna Calvi — award-winning English singer-songwriter and guitarist, mixing indie-rock riffs with soaring ballads. Additional music by Chris Wheeler and Dom Bouffard.

🗣️ Performed in German and English with English supertitles
⏱️ 1 hour 40 minutes, no intermission

blocks.title: Why This Matters
blocks.body:
𝗪𝗛𝗬 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗠𝗔𝗧𝗧𝗘𝗥𝗦

Robert Wilson passed away in 2025. This is one of the last major productions he created — a final statement from an artist who spent five decades redefining what theater could be. His collaborations read like a who's-who of 20th-century art: Philip Glass (Einstein on the Beach), David Byrne, Tom Waits, Lou Reed, Mikhail Baryshnikov.

The pairing with Anna Calvi is inspired — her raw, urgent guitar work against Wilson's glacial visual precision creates something you won't see anywhere else. This is not a conventional adaptation of Moby Dick. It's Wilson's meditation on obsession, rendered in light and sound.

blocks.title: Before & After
blocks.body:
𝗕𝗘𝗙𝗢𝗥𝗘 & 𝗔𝗙𝗧𝗘𝗥

Fort Greene is one of Brooklyn's best dining neighborhoods:
• DeKalb Ave and Fulton St are lined with restaurants
• The BAM area has its own pre-theater energy
• No intermission means plan dinner before, drinks after
• The show runs 1hr 40min — you'll be out by 9:45 if it starts at 8

blocks.title: Check
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗖𝗛𝗘𝗖𝗞

𝗕𝗘𝗦𝗧 𝗧𝗜𝗠𝗘𝗦

• Note: Parsons Dance at The Joyce runs the same dates — possible to pair both in the same trip
